description: An empty catch or the @ operator hides a failure, so a corrupt or insecure state continues silently.
body: Failure mode: a fallible call is wrapped in catch (\Exception $e) {} or prefixed with @, so when it fails the program continues with missing or wrong data and the error never reaches logs or a handler. Detection signals: an empty catch block; the @ suppression operator on a real operation; a function's false/null return ignored; failures that produce no log entry. Do not suppress or swallow errors. Safer fallback: catch only the narrow exception you can handle and rethrow or log the rest, remove @ in favor of checking and throwing, route unrecoverable errors to a central handler, and test that the expected exception type propagates.
